description: Models when Amazon Bedrock On-Demand token pricing should remain in place versus when Provisioned Throughput, model units, or reserved service tiers may reduce steady inference cost.

severity_rules.name: on_demand_above_commitment_breakeven
severity_rules.condition: on_demand_monthly > committed_monthly AND monthly_savings_pct >= 10 AND effective_utilization_pct >= 60 AND traffic_cv <= 0.35
severity_rules.description: Steady token usage may be cheaper on a committed Bedrock throughput or reserved service tier, subject to model and region support.

severity_rules.name: committed_bedrock_capacity_underutilized
severity_rules.description: Bedrock committed throughput or reserved capacity appears underutilized. Review commitment size, routing, and expiration before renewal.

validation_before_change: Confirm model and region support for the selected commitment mode.
Compare p95 and p99 latency against current On-Demand behavior.
Check traffic seasonality and deployment plans before locking a commitment.
Separate input and output token prices; do not use total token count only.
reporting: Show On-Demand monthly cost, committed monthly cost, and break-even arithmetic.
State whether pricing came from cost_report, AWS Pricing MCP, or static estimate.
Report underutilized commitments separately from On-Demand break-even candidates.
